description_long: This is a stone water well with a cylindrical base constructed from large, gray stone blocks, giving it a sturdy and traditional appearance. The well is topped with a wooden gabled roof supported by two vertical wooden posts, providing shelter from above. A horizontal wooden spindle runs between the posts, equipped with a hand crank on one side and a simple handle on the other, suggesting a manual water-drawing mechanism. The interior of the well appears to be open, with a greenish hue indicating the presence of water or a water-like surface inside. The stonework is slightly irregular, adding a rustic charm, while the wood grain on the roof and spindle is clearly visible. There are no visible decorative elements or additional protective barriers beyond the stone wall and roof.
description: A traditional stone water well with a wooden gabled roof and manual crank mechanism, featuring a cylindrical stone base and open water interior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden roof, and the hand crank mechanism prominently on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden post and the gabled roof, with the stone wall's block pattern visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite side of the crank mechanism and the open well interior beneath the roof.
description_view_3: Back view focusing on the wooden post and the rear of the roof, with the crank handle visible on the right.
attribute_well_cover_design: Gabled wooden roof supported by two posts; no lid or slab covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Manual crank and spindle mechanism present, made of wood and metal, mounted horizontally between posts.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Cylindrical stone wall approximately waist-high, forming the main protective barrier.
